name: Brownie
description: Brownies are household spirits from Scottish folklore, said to inhabit homes and farms. They are known for their helpful nature, secretly performing chores and tasks during the night while the human inhabitants sleep. In return for their unseen labor, Brownies traditionally request small offerings of food, such as porridge or milk. However, they are easily offended by gifts of clothing, payment for their services, or perceived slights, which will cause them to vanish and potentially turn into mischievous boggarts. Brownies are generally described as being shy and solitary creatures, preferring to work in the shadows and avoid direct interaction with humans. While they are usually benevolent, their temperament can be unpredictable, and it is important to treat them with respect and gratitude to maintain their goodwill. Their assistance often ensures a smooth-running household and bountiful harvests. Stories about Brownies are common throughout Scotland, varying slightly from region to region, but they all share the common thread of a helpful, albeit reclusive, domestic spirit. They represent a connection to the land and the importance of reciprocity in maintaining a harmonious relationship with the natural world.
subtitle: Helpful household spirit of Scotland
appearance: Brownies are typically described as small, wizened creatures with brown skin and hair, often dressed in rags or wearing no clothing at all. They are generally male and are said to have a hairy or wrinkled appearance. Their height is usually no more than a few feet tall, and they are often described as being similar in appearance to elderly men.
habitat: Brownies inhabit homes, farms, and other domestic settings, typically residing in hidden corners such as attics, cellars, or behind fireplaces. They are most commonly found in rural areas of Scotland.
behavior: Brownies are generally helpful and benevolent, performing chores and tasks during the night to assist the human inhabitants of the house. They are shy and solitary creatures, preferring to work in secret and avoid direct interaction with humans. They are easily offended by gifts of clothing, payment for their services, or perceived slights. They are typically nocturnal.

keyMyths.title: The Brownie's Porridge
keyMyths.description: A farmer left out a bowl of porridge for the Brownie each night as thanks for his help. When the farmer forgot one night, the Brownie disappeared and the farm suffered.

sources.author: Walter Gregor
sources.work: Notes on the Folk-Lore of the North-East of Scotland
